remember: Students will be able to define the concept of atmospheric circulation and weather systems.
Students will be able to identify the factors influencing atmospheric circulation.
Students will be able to recall the types of winds and their characteristics.
Students will be able to describe the basic components of weather systems.
understand: Students will be able to explain the relationship between atmospheric circulation and weather patterns.
Students will be able to describe the role of high and low-pressure systems in shaping weather.
Students will be able to understand the concept of fronts and their impact on weather.
Students will be able to interpret weather maps and charts.
apply: Students will be able to apply their knowledge of atmospheric circulation to predict weather patterns.
Students will be able to analyze the impact of weather systems on different regions and communities.
Students will be able to use their understanding of atmospheric circulation to explain natural disasters such as hurricanes and typhoons.
Students will be able to propose measures to mitigate the effects of extreme weather events.
analyze: Students will be able to analyze the global wind pattern and its impact on regional climates.
Students will be able to evaluate the role of atmospheric circulation in shaping global climate patterns.
Students will be able to compare and contrast different types of weather systems and their effects on the environment.
Students will be able to discuss the human and environmental impacts of extreme weather events.

ncert_objectives: As per NCERT objective 1, to understand the concept of atmospheric circulation and its importance in shaping our climate and weather.
To comprehend the factors that influence atmospheric circulation, including the rotation of the Earth, the uneven heating of the sun, and the presence of mountains and oceans.
To describe the different types of winds, including planetary, monsoon, and local winds.
To explain the formation and characteristics of high and low-pressure systems, fronts, and other weather phenomena.
board_exam_objectives: To test the students' understanding of the basic concepts of atmospheric circulation and weather systems.
To assess the students' ability to analyze and interpret weather maps and charts.
To evaluate the students' knowledge of the factors influencing atmospheric circulation and the impact of weather systems on different regions and communities.
To examine the students' ability to apply their knowledge of atmospheric circulation to real-world scenarios and propose measures to mitigate the effects of extreme weather events.
